Trigger on direct requests ("add a new lambda", "update template.yaml", "add an SQS target to the access manager"), AND proactively whenever Claude is implementing code changes that end up adding or modifying a SAM template.yaml as a side-effect (for example: adding a new Lambda env var, wiring a new event source, adding an IAM permission, introducing a new SAM resource). If the implementation touches template.yaml at all, invoke this skill before writing the change.
- mr-description: Writes merge request message description when raising a GitLab Merge Request. Use when creating a merge request or when user asks to summarise changes for a merge request.
- commit-message: Generate concise git commit messages for Data Engineering changes. Use PROACTIVELY when the user asks to commit files, create a commit, or generate a git commit message.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.

- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the current diff and apply the fixes — equivalent to /code-review --fix.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

input.prompt:
Review all changes on branch `DATA_4715_audit_log_to_s3` against `master` in the repo at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator`.

This is a Python 3.13 AWS Lambda project managed with `uv`, deployed via AWS SAM. It orchestrates GDPR Right to Erasure (RTE) requests. Type checking is done with `basedpyright`.

Steps to follow:
1. Run `git diff master...HEAD` to get all changed files and their diffs.
2. Run `git log master..HEAD --oneline` to see all commits on this branch.
3. Read any changed source files in full for context (especially in `src/` and `tests/`).
4. Run `uv run pytest` to check tests pass.
5. Run `uv run basedpyright` (or equivalent type check command from the project) to check for type errors.

Focus your review on:
- **Correctness**: logic errors, off-by-one, race conditions, wrong assumptions about AWS SDK behaviour
- **Performance**: unnecessary DynamoDB/S3 calls, inefficient retry logic, memory usage in Lambda
- **Security**: IAM over-permissioning, data exposure in logs, S3 bucket policy concerns
- **Test quality**: coverage gaps, missing edge cases, improper mocking of AWS services
- **Type safety**: Any widening, missing TypedDicts, unguarded access on Optional fields
- **Deployment safety**: SAM template changes, environment variable handling, backward compatibility

Project conventions to keep in mind:
- `# pyright: ignore[...]` is last resort — fix the type first
- Never widen return type to `Any` to silence a diagnostic
- Tests use `pytest`; run with `uv run pytest`
- `TODO.md` should be kept up to date

Provide findings grouped by severity (Critical / Major / Minor / Nit), with file path and line numbers for each finding.

---

# Code Review: `DATA_4715_audit_log_to_s3` vs `master`

## Inferred assumptions
- This branch implements the audit-log archival pipeline: requests reaching the terminal `done` state get their full audit trail written to S3 as a single `.jsonl.gz` object, then the DynamoDB entries are deleted; the `GET /status/{id}` reader already falls back to S3.
- A duplicate-`id` POST should return HTTP 409 (regression-tested), and the duplicate-detection contract has been pulled up out of `RequestRepository` implementations into a typed `DuplicateRequestError`.
- Tests pass (173 passing) and `basedpyright` reports `0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes`.

## Files reviewed (all supported languages)

| File | Language |
|---|---|
| `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/audit_logging.py` | Python |
| `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py` | Python |
| `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/processor_lambda.py` | Python |
| `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py` | Python |
| `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_repository.py` | Python |
| `tests/test_audit_logging.py`, `test_creator_lambda.py`, `test_dynamodb_audit_logger.py`, `test_dynamodb_request_repository.py`, `test_request_processor.py`, `test_request_repository.py`, `test_s3_audit_log_writer.py` | Python |
| `template.yaml` | AWS SAM |
| `README.md`, `TODO.md` | Markdown |

---

## Critical

_None._

---

## Major

### M1. `process_phase1` skips archival because it transitions to `phase-2`, never `done`
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py:289-299` and `333-341`

`_archive_if_done` runs after a successful `process_request` and gates archival on `request.state == "done"`. Tracing the production state machine: `process_phase1` transitions to `phase-2`, `process_phase2` raises `NotImplementedError`, and `process_phase3` raises `NotImplementedError` *before* any transition to `done`. **There is no production code path that ever sets `state = "done"`.**

The unit tests pass only because each archive test monkey-patches `process_phase1` to set `state = "done"`. In production, `_archive_if_done` is currently dead code: every successful `process_request` exits in a non-terminal state.

This is a Major issue for two reasons:
1. The README and `TODO.md` advertise that "audit logging materialising to S3 after successful erasure" is done, but no real path exercises it.
2. When `process_phase3` is eventually implemented and ends with `_transition_request(request, "done")`, archival will fire — but the `process_request` call that does the transition will return successfully *and* `request.state` will be `"done"` *for that single sweep iteration only*. On the next sweep, `get_all_in_state("done")` will not be in `PROCESS_STATES`, so the archival hook in `process()` won't run. That means archival is correct only when phase-3 transitions to `done` **inside the same iteration** — which is fine, but please add a regression test that drives a real `phase-3 → done` flow once that handler is implemented, and document explicitly in the docstring of `_archive_if_done` that it relies on `process_request` being the *transitioning* call.

**Recommendation**: Add a comment on `_archive_if_done` calling out the precondition ("only fires when `process_request` transitioned the state to `"done"` in this same iteration"), and add an end-to-end test (still through `process()`) that invokes a real handler implementation transitioning `phase-3 → done`. Also update `TODO.md`: archival code exists but has no live caller — consider re-opening or annotating that line.

---

### M2. S3 IAM policy resource breaks when `AuditLogS3Prefix` is empty
**File**: `template.yaml:201` (and identically at line 227)

```yaml
Resource: !Sub "arn:aws:s3:::${AuditLogS3Bucket}/${AuditLogS3Prefix}/*"
```

The Python code (`audit_logging.py:255`, `312`) accepts an empty prefix and writes objects directly under the bucket root (`{id}.jsonl.gz`). But this IAM policy hard-codes `${AuditLogS3Prefix}/*`. If the parameter is set to `""`, the resulting ARN becomes `arn:aws:s3:::bucket//*`, which **does not match `bucket/req-1.jsonl.gz`** — so PutObject and GetObject will both fail with AccessDenied at runtime. The Lambda will silently log the failure and the audit log will never be archived (or readable) until someone redeploys with a prefix.

**Recommendation** (one of):
- Make `AuditLogS3Prefix` non-empty by contract — add `AllowedPattern: ".+"` and a `MinLength: 1` on the parameter, plus a Description note.
- Or, if empty prefixes must be supported, drop the prefix from the IAM resource and grant on `arn:aws:s3:::${AuditLogS3Bucket}/*` (more permissive but matches the code's tolerance for empty prefix).

The current asymmetry between code (tolerates empty) and IAM (requires non-empty) is the bug.

---

### M3. `audit.log("Erasure request was created")` runs even on duplicate-409 path? — verified clean, but adjacent risk
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py:158-166`

Verified: the `return` on line 160 short-circuits the `audit.log` call on line 165, so duplicates do **not** generate a spurious audit entry. Good.

However: on the dry-run + duplicate path, the code currently logs *no* audit message and returns 200 (because the `request_repo.create` call is skipped under `in_dry_run_mode`). This means a dry-run client cannot detect duplicate-id collisions. If that matters for soak-testing, document it; otherwise add a comment explaining the deliberate skip.

**Severity**: Major (dry-run silently masks duplicates) → reduce to Minor if dry-run is only used for cold-start smoke tests. I cannot tell from the code alone — please confirm intent and add a comment either way.

---

## Minor

### m1. `_archive_if_done` swallows all exceptions — no metric, no alarm
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py:206-212`

The `except Exception:` swallow is intentional and correct (so one stuck archival doesn't fail the sweep), but on a permanent failure (e.g., S3 bucket policy regression) every successful processed request will silently leave its audit log in DynamoDB. There is no CloudWatch metric or alarm on this — only an `ERROR` log line.

**Recommendation**: Either (a) emit a Powertools `Metrics` data-point (`AuditArchivalFailed`) so a CloudWatch alarm can page the team, or (b) at minimum log a structured `extra={"id": request.id}` so operators can filter quickly. The `logger.exception` call already includes the id in the format string, but moving it to structured fields makes log-based alerting easier.

### m2. `process_phase3` raises before transitioning to `done` — dead branch in `_transition_request("done", ...)` semantics
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py:333-341`

Same root cause as M1, but worth its own line: the `# Finally transition to done` comment at line 340 is misleading because the very next line raises `NotImplementedError` unconditionally. Recommend restructuring to a clear `# TODO: transition to done once phase-3 logic lands` to avoid future readers thinking the handler is "almost done."

### m3. `delete_logs` queries with `ScanIndexForward` defaulting to True — wasted RCUs
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/audit_logging.py:165-186`

`delete_logs` only needs the keys, not the items in any particular order. The query already uses `ProjectionExpression="#id, #ts"` which is correct. Setting `ScanIndexForward=False` (or just leaving the default) makes no functional difference here, but consider adding `ConsistentRead=False` (the default — ok) and a comment that the `get_logs` call in `AuditArchiver.archive` is the canonical "read everything" path; `delete_logs` is intentionally light-weight. Not a bug, but worth a one-line docstring update so future maintainers don't conflate the two query paths.

### m4. `_archive_if_done` runs *after* `successful_request_count += 1` — counter is correct, but failure to archive does not decrement it
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py:140-141`

`successful_request_count` reflects "requests whose state transition succeeded," which is the right semantic — archival is a side-effect, not part of the contract. Just make sure the team understands this distinction; the README's "successfully processed and reaches the terminal `done` state" phrasing could be read both ways. Consider adding a one-liner to the README's "Audit log lifecycle" section: "archival is best-effort and idempotent; failure does not affect request processing status."

### m5. `delete_logs` paginated query and BatchWrite use `ExpressionAttributeNames` for `#ts` even when `ProjectionExpression` is the only place it appears — slight maintainability nit
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/audit_logging.py:170-176`

Both `#id` and `#ts` are declared in `ExpressionAttributeNames` to avoid reserved-keyword collisions. `id` is not reserved in DynamoDB; `timestamp` is. So the `#id` aliasing is unnecessary defensive programming. Either remove `#id` aliasing or keep both for symmetry — your call. Low impact.

### m6. `creator_lambda` audit log on creation is fire-and-forget after the 200 response is decided
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py:165-166`

If `audit.log` raises (DynamoDB hiccup), the lambda returns 500 to the caller even though the request *was* persisted. That's misleading: the caller will probably retry, hit the new 409 path, and conclude their request "failed." Two options:
- Wrap `audit.log` in a try/except + warn-log (preferred), so a successful create always returns 200 even if the audit hiccups.
- Leave as-is and document the contract.

Currently the original behavior is preserved (this PR didn't introduce the issue), but the new 409 path makes this latent bug easier to trigger because clients will retry on 5xx and now get a deterministic 409 they can't reconcile with a 200 they never received.

### m7. Test redundancy — `tests/test_audit_logging.py` `_make_dynamo_pages_with_keys` duplicated structure
**File**: `tests/test_audit_logging.py:609-622`

The four `delete_logs_*` tests build very similar `pages` and assertion shapes. They could be parametrized over `(timestamps, expected_batch_count, expected_calls)`, collapsing four tests into one parametrized test with three cases. Optional cleanup — the redundancy isn't masking missing coverage.

### m8. `tests/test_request_processor.py::test_archiver_exception_does_not_propagate` doesn't assert the swallow
**File**: `tests/test_request_processor.py:1299-1307`

The test calls `proc.process()` and relies on no exception escaping. Add an assertion that `archiver_mock.archive` was actually called — otherwise the test passes vacuously if some refactor accidentally skips the archive call entirely.

```python
cast(MagicMock, archiver_mock.archive).assert_called_once_with("req-archive")
```

### m9. SAM: `ProcessS3WritePolicy` and `ReporterS3ReadPolicy` mutate the role created by `Custom::ServerlessAccessManager`
**File**: `template.yaml:192-203`, `218-229`

Per the `serverless-access-manager` rules, attaching an `AWS::IAM::Policy` (an inline-style attached policy) to a role owned by the custom resource is borderline — the custom resource owns the role and reconciles its permissions. The rule of thumb is "no managed policies or inline `Policies:` on the same Lambda's execution role." `AWS::IAM::Policy` attached via `Roles: [...]` is technically a separate resource, so it survives reconciliation, but it does mean two systems write to the same role. If the team's `serverless-access-manager` later strips unknown attached policies, this S3 access disappears.

**Recommendation**: Confirm with the platform team that attached `AWS::IAM::Policy` resources are tolerated by the reconciliation loop. If not, add an `S3` *target* type to the custom resource's `Targets` block once it supports S3 buckets, or use a separate execution role. If yes, leave a comment in the template explaining the contract so future maintainers don't get surprised.

### m10. `AuditLogS3Bucket` and `AuditLogS3Prefix` parameters lack `Type` precision
**File**: `template.yaml:27-32`

Both parameters are `Type: String` with no `AllowedPattern`, `MinLength`, or `Description` describing format. As called out in M2, the prefix is allowed to be empty by code but forbidden by the IAM ARN. Tighten:

```yaml
AuditLogS3Bucket:
  Description: ...
  Type: String
  MinLength: 3
  MaxLength: 63
  AllowedPattern: "^[a-z0-9.-]+$"
AuditLogS3Prefix:
  Description: Key prefix (no leading or trailing slash). Required.
  Type: String
  MinLength: 1
  AllowedPattern: "^[^/].*[^/]$|^[^/]+$"
```

### m11. `_archive_if_done`: dry-run silently no-ops with no log line
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py:202-203`

When `dry_run` is True, the early return prints nothing. Other dry-run branches in this class (`_transition_request`, `process_request`) emit explicit `DryRun: …` log lines. Consider adding `logger.info("DryRun: would archive audit log for %s", request.id)` for symmetry and visibility during pre-prod soak tests.

---

## Nit

### n1. `gzip.compress` produces non-deterministic output (mtime) — fine, but worth knowing
`gzip.compress(...)` writes the current `mtime` into the header by default, so two writes of the same content produce different bytes. This is irrelevant for correctness, but if anyone ever depends on object hash equality across rewrites (e.g., S3 ETag-based skip-write optimisations), pass `mtime=0` to `gzip.GzipFile`. Pure trivia.

### n2. Magic constant `0.1` and `2.0` in backoff are well-named — Pass
The `_backoff_seconds` helper, `BACKOFF_BASE_SECONDS`, and `BACKOFF_MAX_SECONDS` are an exemplary fix for the cryptic-construct rule called out in the system prompt's worked example. No action needed.

### n3. `request_repository.py:106`: typo — "does not already exists" should be "does not already exist"
Pre-existing; flagging while in the area.

### n4. `audit_logging.py:9`: `cast` import is now used by `_batch_write_with_retry`
Just confirming the import stays relevant. Pass.

### n5. README — `POST /erasure-request` example uses literal API ID
**File**: `README.md:37`

`omqlg03jma.execute-api.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/prod/erasure-request` is a real-looking API Gateway ID. If that's a production endpoint, redact to `<api-id>` in the README to avoid presenting it as a "go-poke-here" target. If it's a placeholder, comment it.

### n6. `tests/test_dynamodb_audit_logger.py` region change `us-east-1 → eu-west-1`
Pleasing alignment with the eu-west-1 deployment region. Pass.

### n7. `_archive_if_done` and `archive` both log "Audit log for id … archived" — duplicate log line
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/audit_logging.py:349` and `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py` (no log on success)

`AuditArchiver.archive` already logs the success line. `_archive_if_done` only logs on failure. So the only duplication is internal to `archive` (line 322 logs each S3 put, line 349 logs the overall outcome). That's actually two distinct events — fine. No action.

---

## Summary

### Overall assessment
This is a high-quality changeset. The split into `S3AuditLogWriter`, `AuditArchiver`, and the retry/backoff helpers is clean; type annotations are precise (no `Any` widening, no inappropriate `# pyright: ignore`), and the test suite is thorough — particularly the moto-backed round-trip in `test_s3_audit_log_writer.py` and the resilience test that verifies DynamoDB entries survive an S3 failure. The duplicate-id refactor (`DuplicateRequestError`) is the right shape: typed, decoupled from `botocore.ClientError`, and covered for both implementations.

The two real problems are deployment-time: **(M1)** archival is currently unreachable in production code paths because no handler transitions to `done`, and **(M2)** the IAM resource ARN breaks if `AuditLogS3Prefix` is empty even though the Python tolerates empty. Both are fixable in a small follow-up.

### Priority action list
1. **M2** — Constrain `AuditLogS3Prefix` to non-empty (or relax the IAM ARN). One-line fix; prevents a runtime AccessDenied that's easy to ship and hard to spot.
2. **M1** — Add a regression test driving a real phase-3 → done transition once the handler exists, and update `TODO.md` to reflect that the *plumbing* for archival is in but the *trigger* (phase-3 implementation) is still pending.
3. **m1** — Emit a CloudWatch metric on `_archive_if_done` failures so silent archive failures alert someone.
4. **m6** — Wrap the post-create `audit.log` call in a try/except so a successful create cannot return 500.
5. **m9** — Confirm with the platform team that attached `AWS::IAM::Policy` resources survive `serverless-access-manager` reconciliation; add a template comment either way.

### Positive observations
- The retry helper extraction (`_batch_write_with_retry`, `_backoff_seconds`, named `BACKOFF_*` constants) is a textbook example of the inline-helper-extraction guidance — directly addresses the cryptic-construct rule.
- `DuplicateRequestError` decouples the API-layer 409 contract from the DynamoDB error code, and is correctly tested in both `InMemoryRequestRepository` and `DynamoDBRequestRepository`.
- The fix at `request_processor.py:245` (`message = message + " due to: " + reason`) corrects a pre-existing typo (`message = message = " due to: " + reason`) that would have silently dropped the reason text. Good catch.
- Type discipline is excellent — `WriteRequestTypeDef` introduced for batch writes, `cast` used at exactly one boundary, ID lookups still go through the documented `# pyright: ignore[reportTypedDictNotRequiredAccess]` pattern.
- `S3AuditLogWriter` sorts entries by timestamp, fixing the chronological-ordering invariant that `S3AuditLogReader` and `FallbackAuditLogReader` rely on.
- README has a complete and accurate description of the new endpoint, audit lifecycle, and process-Lambda environment variables.

### Scorecard

| Dimension | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Formatting compliance | Pass | Python (black/isort), SAM YAML — clean |
| Type precision | Pass | No `Any` widening; one well-justified `cast` for `UnprocessedItems`; `# pyright: ignore` only on the documented TypedDict-access pattern |
| Test redundancy | Minor | One cluster of four tests in `test_audit_logging.py` could be parametrized (m7); `test_archiver_exception_does_not_propagate` is missing one assertion (m8) |
| README sync | In sync | New endpoint, audit lifecycle, and env vars all documented |
| Paradigm adherence | Pass | Clean composition (`AuditArchiver` composes `DynamoDBAuditLogger` + `S3AuditLogWriter`); no inheritance abuse |
| Extensibility risk | Low | Adding new archival sinks is a matter of new `S3AuditLogWriter`-shaped classes; `AuditArchiver` is the seam |
| Inline-logic clarity | Pass | `_batch_write_with_retry` and `_backoff_seconds` are the right granularity |

### Files referenced (absolute paths)
- `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/audit_logging.py`
- `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py`

Review all changes on branch `DATA_4715_audit_log_to_s3` against `master` in the repo at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator`.

This is a Python 3.13 AWS Lambda project managed with `uv`, deployed via AWS SAM. It orchestrates GDPR Right to Erasure (RTE) requests. Type checking is done with `basedpyright`.

Steps to follow:
1. Run `git diff master...HEAD` to get all changed files and their diffs.
2. Run `git log master..HEAD --oneline` to see all commits on this branch.
3. Read any changed source files in full for context (especially in `src/` and `tests/`).
4. Run `uv run pytest` to check tests pass.
5. Run `uv run basedpyright` (or equivalent type check command from the project) to check for type errors.

Focus your review on:
- **Correctness**: logic errors, off-by-one, race conditions, wrong assumptions about AWS SDK behaviour
- **Performance**: unnecessary DynamoDB/S3 calls, inefficient retry logic, memory usage in Lambda
- **Security**: IAM over-permissioning, data exposure in logs, S3 bucket policy concerns
- **Test quality**: coverage gaps, missing edge cases, improper mocking of AWS services
- **Type safety**: Any widening, missing TypedDicts, unguarded access on Optional fields
- **Deployment safety**: SAM template changes, environment variable handling, backward compatibility

Project conventions to keep in mind:
- `# pyright: ignore[...]` is last resort — fix the type first
- Never widen return type to `Any` to silence a diagnostic
- Tests use `pytest`; run with `uv run pytest`
- `TODO.md` should be kept up to date

Provide findings grouped by severity (Critical / Major / Minor / Nit), with file path and line numbers for each finding.

# Code Review: `DATA_4715_audit_log_to_s3` vs `master`

## Inferred assumptions
- This branch implements the audit-log archival pipeline: requests reaching the terminal `done` state get their full audit trail written to S3 as a single `.jsonl.gz` object, then the DynamoDB entries are deleted; the `GET /status/{id}` reader already falls back to S3.
- A duplicate-`id` POST should return HTTP 409 (regression-tested), and the duplicate-detection contract has been pulled up out of `RequestRepository` implementations into a typed `DuplicateRequestError`.
- Tests pass (173 passing) and `basedpyright` reports `0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes`.

## Files reviewed (all supported languages)

| File | Language |
|---|---|
| `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/audit_logging.py` | Python |
| `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py` | Python |
| `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/processor_lambda.py` | Python |
| `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py` | Python |
| `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_repository.py` | Python |
| `tests/test_audit_logging.py`, `test_creator_lambda.py`, `test_dynamodb_audit_logger.py`, `test_dynamodb_request_repository.py`, `test_request_processor.py`, `test_request_repository.py`, `test_s3_audit_log_writer.py` | Python |
| `template.yaml` | AWS SAM |
| `README.md`, `TODO.md` | Markdown |

---

## Critical

_None._

---

## Major

### M1. `process_phase1` skips archival because it transitions to `phase-2`, never `done`
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py:289-299` and `333-341`

`_archive_if_done` runs after a successful `process_request` and gates archival on `request.state == "done"`. Tracing the production state machine: `process_phase1` transitions to `phase-2`, `process_phase2` raises `NotImplementedError`, and `process_phase3` raises `NotImplementedError` *before* any transition to `done`. **There is no production code path that ever sets `state = "done"`.**

The unit tests pass only because each archive test monkey-patches `process_phase1` to set `state = "done"`. In production, `_archive_if_done` is currently dead code: every successful `process_request` exits in a non-terminal state.

This is a Major issue for two reasons:
1. The README and `TODO.md` advertise that "audit logging materialising to S3 after successful erasure" is done, but no real path exercises it.
2. When `process_phase3` is eventually implemented and ends with `_transition_request(request, "done")`, archival will fire — but the `process_request` call that does the transition will return successfully *and* `request.state` will be `"done"` *for that single sweep iteration only*. On the next sweep, `get_all_in_state("done")` will not be in `PROCESS_STATES`, so the archival hook in `process()` won't run. That means archival is correct only when phase-3 transitions to `done` **inside the same iteration** — which is fine, but please add a regression test that drives a real `phase-3 → done` flow once that handler is implemented, and document explicitly in the docstring of `_archive_if_done` that it relies on `process_request` being the *transitioning* call.

**Recommendation**: Add a comment on `_archive_if_done` calling out the precondition ("only fires when `process_request` transitioned the state to `"done"` in this same iteration"), and add an end-to-end test (still through `process()`) that invokes a real handler implementation transitioning `phase-3 → done`. Also update `TODO.md`: archival code exists but has no live caller — consider re-opening or annotating that line.

---

### M2. S3 IAM policy resource breaks when `AuditLogS3Prefix` is empty
**File**: `template.yaml:201` (and identically at line 227)

```yaml
Resource: !Sub "arn:aws:s3:::${AuditLogS3Bucket}/${AuditLogS3Prefix}/*"
```

The Python code (`audit_logging.py:255`, `312`) accepts an empty prefix and writes objects directly under the bucket root (`{id}.jsonl.gz`). But this IAM policy hard-codes `${AuditLogS3Prefix}/*`. If the parameter is set to `""`, the resulting ARN becomes `arn:aws:s3:::bucket//*`, which **does not match `bucket/req-1.jsonl.gz`** — so PutObject and GetObject will both fail with AccessDenied at runtime. The Lambda will silently log the failure and the audit log will never be archived (or readable) until someone redeploys with a prefix.

**Recommendation** (one of):
- Make `AuditLogS3Prefix` non-empty by contract — add `AllowedPattern: ".+"` and a `MinLength: 1` on the parameter, plus a Description note.
- Or, if empty prefixes must be supported, drop the prefix from the IAM resource and grant on `arn:aws:s3:::${AuditLogS3Bucket}/*` (more permissive but matches the code's tolerance for empty prefix).

The current asymmetry between code (tolerates empty) and IAM (requires non-empty) is the bug.

---

### M3. `audit.log("Erasure request was created")` runs even on duplicate-409 path? — verified clean, but adjacent risk
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py:158-166`

Verified: the `return` on line 160 short-circuits the `audit.log` call on line 165, so duplicates do **not** generate a spurious audit entry. Good.

However: on the dry-run + duplicate path, the code currently logs *no* audit message and returns 200 (because the `request_repo.create` call is skipped under `in_dry_run_mode`). This means a dry-run client cannot detect duplicate-id collisions. If that matters for soak-testing, document it; otherwise add a comment explaining the deliberate skip.

**Severity**: Major (dry-run silently masks duplicates) → reduce to Minor if dry-run is only used for cold-start smoke tests. I cannot tell from the code alone — please confirm intent and add a comment either way.

---

## Minor

### m1. `_archive_if_done` swallows all exceptions — no metric, no alarm
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py:206-212`

The `except Exception:` swallow is intentional and correct (so one stuck archival doesn't fail the sweep), but on a permanent failure (e.g., S3 bucket policy regression) every successful processed request will silently leave its audit log in DynamoDB. There is no CloudWatch metric or alarm on this — only an `ERROR` log line.

**Recommendation**: Either (a) emit a Powertools `Metrics` data-point (`AuditArchivalFailed`) so a CloudWatch alarm can page the team, or (b) at minimum log a structured `extra={"id": request.id}` so operators can filter quickly. The `logger.exception` call already includes the id in the format string, but moving it to structured fields makes log-based alerting easier.

### m2. `process_phase3` raises before transitioning to `done` — dead branch in `_transition_request("done", ...)` semantics
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py:333-341`

Same root cause as M1, but worth its own line: the `# Finally transition to done` comment at line 340 is misleading because the very next line raises `NotImplementedError` unconditionally. Recommend restructuring to a clear `# TODO: transition to done once phase-3 logic lands` to avoid future readers thinking the handler is "almost done."

### m3. `delete_logs` queries with `ScanIndexForward` defaulting to True — wasted RCUs
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/audit_logging.py:165-186`

`delete_logs` only needs the keys, not the items in any particular order. The query already uses `ProjectionExpression="#id, #ts"` which is correct. Setting `ScanIndexForward=False` (or just leaving the default) makes no functional difference here, but consider adding `ConsistentRead=False` (the default — ok) and a comment that the `get_logs` call in `AuditArchiver.archive` is the canonical "read everything" path; `delete_logs` is intentionally light-weight. Not a bug, but worth a one-line docstring update so future maintainers don't conflate the two query paths.

### m4. `_archive_if_done` runs *after* `successful_request_count += 1` — counter is correct, but failure to archive does not decrement it
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py:140-141`

`successful_request_count` reflects "requests whose state transition succeeded," which is the right semantic — archival is a side-effect, not part of the contract. Just make sure the team understands this distinction; the README's "successfully processed and reaches the terminal `done` state" phrasing could be read both ways. Consider adding a one-liner to the README's "Audit log lifecycle" section: "archival is best-effort and idempotent; failure does not affect request processing status."

### m5. `delete_logs` paginated query and BatchWrite use `ExpressionAttributeNames` for `#ts` even when `ProjectionExpression` is the only place it appears — slight maintainability nit
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/audit_logging.py:170-176`

Both `#id` and `#ts` are declared in `ExpressionAttributeNames` to avoid reserved-keyword collisions. `id` is not reserved in DynamoDB; `timestamp` is. So the `#id` aliasing is unnecessary defensive programming. Either remove `#id` aliasing or keep both for symmetry — your call. Low impact.

### m6. `creator_lambda` audit log on creation is fire-and-forget after the 200 response is decided
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py:165-166`

If `audit.log` raises (DynamoDB hiccup), the lambda returns 500 to the caller even though the request *was* persisted. That's misleading: the caller will probably retry, hit the new 409 path, and conclude their request "failed." Two options:
- Wrap `audit.log` in a try/except + warn-log (preferred), so a successful create always returns 200 even if the audit hiccups.
- Leave as-is and document the contract.

Currently the original behavior is preserved (this PR didn't introduce the issue), but the new 409 path makes this latent bug easier to trigger because clients will retry on 5xx and now get a deterministic 409 they can't reconcile with a 200 they never received.

### m7. Test redundancy — `tests/test_audit_logging.py` `_make_dynamo_pages_with_keys` duplicated structure
**File**: `tests/test_audit_logging.py:609-622`

The four `delete_logs_*` tests build very similar `pages` and assertion shapes. They could be parametrized over `(timestamps, expected_batch_count, expected_calls)`, collapsing four tests into one parametrized test with three cases. Optional cleanup — the redundancy isn't masking missing coverage.

### m8. `tests/test_request_processor.py::test_archiver_exception_does_not_propagate` doesn't assert the swallow
**File**: `tests/test_request_processor.py:1299-1307`

The test calls `proc.process()` and relies on no exception escaping. Add an assertion that `archiver_mock.archive` was actually called — otherwise the test passes vacuously if some refactor accidentally skips the archive call entirely.

```python
cast(MagicMock, archiver_mock.archive).assert_called_once_with("req-archive")
```

### m9. SAM: `ProcessS3WritePolicy` and `ReporterS3ReadPolicy` mutate the role created by `Custom::ServerlessAccessManager`
**File**: `template.yaml:192-203`, `218-229`

Per the `serverless-access-manager` rules, attaching an `AWS::IAM::Policy` (an inline-style attached policy) to a role owned by the custom resource is borderline — the custom resource owns the role and reconciles its permissions. The rule of thumb is "no managed policies or inline `Policies:` on the same Lambda's execution role." `AWS::IAM::Policy` attached via `Roles: [...]` is technically a separate resource, so it survives reconciliation, but it does mean two systems write to the same role. If the team's `serverless-access-manager` later strips unknown attached policies, this S3 access disappears.

**Recommendation**: Confirm with the platform team that attached `AWS::IAM::Policy` resources are tolerated by the reconciliation loop. If not, add an `S3` *target* type to the custom resource's `Targets` block once it supports S3 buckets, or use a separate execution role. If yes, leave a comment in the template explaining the contract so future maintainers don't get surprised.

### m10. `AuditLogS3Bucket` and `AuditLogS3Prefix` parameters lack `Type` precision
**File**: `template.yaml:27-32`

Both parameters are `Type: String` with no `AllowedPattern`, `MinLength`, or `Description` describing format. As called out in M2, the prefix is allowed to be empty by code but forbidden by the IAM ARN. Tighten:

```yaml
AuditLogS3Bucket:
  Description: ...
  Type: String
  MinLength: 3
  MaxLength: 63
  AllowedPattern: "^[a-z0-9.-]+$"
AuditLogS3Prefix:
  Description: Key prefix (no leading or trailing slash). Required.
  Type: String
  MinLength: 1
  AllowedPattern: "^[^/].*[^/]$|^[^/]+$"
```

### m11. `_archive_if_done`: dry-run silently no-ops with no log line
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py:202-203`

When `dry_run` is True, the early return prints nothing. Other dry-run branches in this class (`_transition_request`, `process_request`) emit explicit `DryRun: …` log lines. Consider adding `logger.info("DryRun: would archive audit log for %s", request.id)` for symmetry and visibility during pre-prod soak tests.

---

## Nit

### n1. `gzip.compress` produces non-deterministic output (mtime) — fine, but worth knowing
`gzip.compress(...)` writes the current `mtime` into the header by default, so two writes of the same content produce different bytes. This is irrelevant for correctness, but if anyone ever depends on object hash equality across rewrites (e.g., S3 ETag-based skip-write optimisations), pass `mtime=0` to `gzip.GzipFile`. Pure trivia.

### n2. Magic constant `0.1` and `2.0` in backoff are well-named — Pass
The `_backoff_seconds` helper, `BACKOFF_BASE_SECONDS`, and `BACKOFF_MAX_SECONDS` are an exemplary fix for the cryptic-construct rule called out in the system prompt's worked example. No action needed.

### n3. `request_repository.py:106`: typo — "does not already exists" should be "does not already exist"
Pre-existing; flagging while in the area.

### n4. `audit_logging.py:9`: `cast` import is now used by `_batch_write_with_retry`
Just confirming the import stays relevant. Pass.

### n5. README — `POST /erasure-request` example uses literal API ID
**File**: `README.md:37`

`omqlg03jma.execute-api.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/prod/erasure-request` is a real-looking API Gateway ID. If that's a production endpoint, redact to `<api-id>` in the README to avoid presenting it as a "go-poke-here" target. If it's a placeholder, comment it.

### n6. `tests/test_dynamodb_audit_logger.py` region change `us-east-1 → eu-west-1`
Pleasing alignment with the eu-west-1 deployment region. Pass.

### n7. `_archive_if_done` and `archive` both log "Audit log for id … archived" — duplicate log line
**File**: `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/audit_logging.py:349` and `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py` (no log on success)

`AuditArchiver.archive` already logs the success line. `_archive_if_done` only logs on failure. So the only duplication is internal to `archive` (line 322 logs each S3 put, line 349 logs the overall outcome). That's actually two distinct events — fine. No action.

---

## Summary

### Overall assessment
This is a high-quality changeset. The split into `S3AuditLogWriter`, `AuditArchiver`, and the retry/backoff helpers is clean; type annotations are precise (no `Any` widening, no inappropriate `# pyright: ignore`), and the test suite is thorough — particularly the moto-backed round-trip in `test_s3_audit_log_writer.py` and the resilience test that verifies DynamoDB entries survive an S3 failure. The duplicate-id refactor (`DuplicateRequestError`) is the right shape: typed, decoupled from `botocore.ClientError`, and covered for both implementations.

The two real problems are deployment-time: **(M1)** archival is currently unreachable in production code paths because no handler transitions to `done`, and **(M2)** the IAM resource ARN breaks if `AuditLogS3Prefix` is empty even though the Python tolerates empty. Both are fixable in a small follow-up.

### Priority action list
1. **M2** — Constrain `AuditLogS3Prefix` to non-empty (or relax the IAM ARN). One-line fix; prevents a runtime AccessDenied that's easy to ship and hard to spot.
2. **M1** — Add a regression test driving a real phase-3 → done transition once the handler exists, and update `TODO.md` to reflect that the *plumbing* for archival is in but the *trigger* (phase-3 implementation) is still pending.
3. **m1** — Emit a CloudWatch metric on `_archive_if_done` failures so silent archive failures alert someone.
4. **m6** — Wrap the post-create `audit.log` call in a try/except so a successful create cannot return 500.
5. **m9** — Confirm with the platform team that attached `AWS::IAM::Policy` resources survive `serverless-access-manager` reconciliation; add a template comment either way.

### Positive observations
- The retry helper extraction (`_batch_write_with_retry`, `_backoff_seconds`, named `BACKOFF_*` constants) is a textbook example of the inline-helper-extraction guidance — directly addresses the cryptic-construct rule.
- `DuplicateRequestError` decouples the API-layer 409 contract from the DynamoDB error code, and is correctly tested in both `InMemoryRequestRepository` and `DynamoDBRequestRepository`.
- The fix at `request_processor.py:245` (`message = message + " due to: " + reason`) corrects a pre-existing typo (`message = message = " due to: " + reason`) that would have silently dropped the reason text. Good catch.
- Type discipline is excellent — `WriteRequestTypeDef` introduced for batch writes, `cast` used at exactly one boundary, ID lookups still go through the documented `# pyright: ignore[reportTypedDictNotRequiredAccess]` pattern.
- `S3AuditLogWriter` sorts entries by timestamp, fixing the chronological-ordering invariant that `S3AuditLogReader` and `FallbackAuditLogReader` rely on.
- README has a complete and accurate description of the new endpoint, audit lifecycle, and process-Lambda environment variables.

### Scorecard

| Dimension | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Formatting compliance | Pass | Python (black/isort), SAM YAML — clean |
| Type precision | Pass | No `Any` widening; one well-justified `cast` for `UnprocessedItems`; `# pyright: ignore` only on the documented TypedDict-access pattern |
| Test redundancy | Minor | One cluster of four tests in `test_audit_logging.py` could be parametrized (m7); `test_archiver_exception_does_not_propagate` is missing one assertion (m8) |
| README sync | In sync | New endpoint, audit lifecycle, and env vars all documented |
| Paradigm adherence | Pass | Clean composition (`AuditArchiver` composes `DynamoDBAuditLogger` + `S3AuditLogWriter`); no inheritance abuse |
| Extensibility risk | Low | Adding new archival sinks is a matter of new `S3AuditLogWriter`-shaped classes; `AuditArchiver` is the seam |
| Inline-logic clarity | Pass | `_batch_write_with_retry` and `_backoff_seconds` are the right granularity |

### Files referenced (absolute paths)
- `/Users/saurabhtyagi/Documents/workspace/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/audit_logging.py`

content.text:
Here's the full review from the deep-code-reviewer. All 173 tests pass and `basedpyright` is clean. Two major issues to address before merging:

---

## Critical
None.

---

## Major

**M1. `_archive_if_done` is currently dead code in production**
`request_processor.py` — No production code path ever transitions a request to `"done"` (phase-3 raises `NotImplementedError` before any transition). The tests pass only because they monkey-patch `process_phase1` to set `state = "done"`. The archival plumbing is correct, but it has no live caller. Update `TODO.md` to reflect that the *trigger* is still pending, and add a regression test once phase-3 is implemented.

**M2. IAM resource ARN breaks if `AuditLogS3Prefix` is empty (`template.yaml:201, 227`)**
The policy ARN is `arn:aws:s3:::${bucket}/${prefix}/*`. If the prefix is empty (which the Python code tolerates), the ARN becomes `bucket//*` and PutObject/GetObject silently fail with AccessDenied. Fix: add `MinLength: 1` to the `AuditLogS3Prefix` parameter, or relax the ARN to `${bucket}/*`.

**M3. `audit.log` in `creator_lambda.py:165` can return 500 after a successful create**
If `audit.log` throws (DynamoDB hiccup), the lambda returns 500 even though the request was persisted. The client retries, gets a 409, and can't reconcile with the 200 it never received. Wrap the `audit.log` call in try/except + warn-log so a successful create always returns 200.

---

## Minor (selected highlights)

- **m1** — `_archive_if_done` swallows all exceptions with no CloudWatch metric. Add a `Metrics` data-point (`AuditArchivalFailed`) or at least structured logging with `id` so an alarm can be wired.
- **m8** — `test_archiver_exception_does_not_propagate` passes vacuously if archival is accidentally skipped. Add `archiver_mock.archive.assert_called_once_with("req-archive")`.
- **m9** — `AWS::IAM::Policy` attached to the `serverless-access-manager`-owned role may be stripped during reconciliation. Confirm with the platform team and add a comment.
- **m11** — Dry-run path in `_archive_if_done` emits no log line, unlike other dry-run branches in the same class.
- **n5** — `README.md:37` contains a real-looking API Gateway ID — redact to `<api-id>`.

---

## Positives

- `DuplicateRequestError` decoupling from `botocore.ClientError` is the right shape.
- `_batch_write_with_retry` / `_backoff_seconds` with named `BACKOFF_*` constants is clean.
- `S3AuditLogWriter` sorts entries by timestamp, fixing the ordering invariant.
- A previously silent bug (`message = message = " due to: "` double-assignment) was caught and fixed.
- Type discipline is excellent throughout — no `Any` widening, `cast` used at exactly one boundary.

**Top 3 actions**: (1) Fix the IAM ARN / prefix constraint (M2 — one-liner, prevents runtime AccessDenied), (2) annotate `_archive_if_done` that archival has no live trigger yet (M1), (3) wrap `audit.log` in creator lambda in try/except (M3).

### `POST /erasure-request`

Creates a new erasure request and queues it for processing.

```sh
curl -k -sS -X POST \
  'https://omqlg03jma.execute-api.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/prod/erasure-request' \
  -H 'x-api-key: <api-key>' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "id": "<unique-id>",
    "source": "onetrust",
    "date_issued": "2026-01-01T12:00:00+00:00",
    "erasure_details": { "email": "user@example.com" }
  }'
```

Required fields: `id`, `source`, `date_issued` (ISO-8601), `erasure_details`
(object with keys from `email`, `first-name`, `last-name`, `postcode`,

case "new":
                self.process_new(request)
            case "in-progress":
                self.process_in_progress(request)
            case "phase-1":
                self.process_phase1(request)
            case "phase-2":
                self.process_phase2(request)
            case "phase-3":
                self.process_phase3(request)
            case _:
                raise NotImplementedError(
                    f'Request processing of state "{request_state}" not implemented'
                )

    def _archive_if_done(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        """Archive the audit log to S3 if the request has reached a terminal "done" state.

        Runs after a successful ``process_request`` only. Archive failures are
        swallowed and logged so they do not fail the surrounding ``process``
        sweep — the next invocation will retry from DynamoDB.
        """
        if self.dry_run or self.archiver is None:
            return
        if request.state != "done":
            return
        try:
            self.archiver.archive(request.id)
        except Exception:
            logger.exception(
                "Failed to archive audit log for request %s; will be retried on next invocation",
                request.id,
            )

    @classmethod
    def _validate_request(
        cls, current_state: ErasureState, request: ErasureRequest
    ) -> None:
        """
        Validate a request for the given current state.

        This will raise an exception if the request is not valid.
        """
        if request.state != current_state:
            raise WrongStateForProcess(request, current_state)

    def _transition_request(
        self,
        request: ErasureRequest,
        new_state: ErasureState,
        reason: str | None = None,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Helper function to transition a request to a new state.

        You can provide an optional reason that will be used in audit logging.
        """
        logger.info("Transitioning request %s to %s", request.id, new_state)
        request.state = new_state
        if self.dry_run:
            logger.info("DryRun: Would update repository")
        else:
            self.repo.update(request)
        message = f"Request was transitioned to {new_state}"
        if reason:
            message = message + " due to: " + reason
        if not self.dry_run:
            self.audit.log(request.id, message)

    def process_new(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        self._validate_request("new", request)
        logger.info("Processing new request %s", request.id)

        # Check if there is an email attached to the request
        email: str | None = request.erasure_details.get("email")

        if email is None or email.strip() == "":
            # Missing email address, this should always be present as the data
            # privacy team verify this in OneTrust before the request reaches
            # our system

            logger.warning(
                "Request %s has no email so will be transitioned to need-more-detail",
                request.id,
            )
            # Update the request before raising an exception
            self._transition_request(request, "need-more-detail", "no email address")
            raise MissingEmailAddress(request)  # TODO: Should we raise?

        # TODO: Update OneTrust in some way to tell it that we've started to
        # process a given request

        self._transition_request(request, "in-progress")

    def process_in_progress(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        self._validate_request("in-progress", request)

        # TODO: In the original process this "in-progress" stage did not do a
        # great deal

        # Enrich the request if possible or required
        self.enrich_request(request)

        # TODO: Update OneTrust in some way to tell it that we've moved to the
        # next phase

        # Move to the next state
        self._transition_request(request, "phase-1")

    def process_phase1(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        self._validate_request("phase-1", request)
        logger.info("Running request %s through Phase-1", request.id)
        # This is the legacy system stage
        # For now all this should do is notify the AS400 team via both Slack
        # AND Microsoft Teams
        self.notify_as400_team(request)

        # TODO: Update OneTrust that phase-1 was done

        self._transition_request(request, "phase-2")

    def process_phase2(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        # This is where the core of erasure logic happened in Tines
        self._validate_request("phase-2", request)

        # TODO: Update OneTrust throughout each part of phase-2

        # TODO: Subflow 1 from Tines:
        # TODO: Search for the customer in All Sites using the Customer API
        # TODO: Submit an erasure to the Customer Erasures API
        # If we found the sites for a customer we include those, otherwise it
        # is not included

        # TODO: Subflow 2 from Tines:
        # TODO: Check the Customer Erasures API Status for the Erasure Request
        # If it's successful we then transition to the next subflow

        # TODO: Subflow 3 from Tines:
        # TODO: Search and Delete Zendesk Tickets

        # TODO: Subflow 4 from Tines:
        # TODO: Search and delete Zendesk users

        # TODO: Subflow 5 from Tines:
        # TODO: Purge the deleted Zendesk tickets

        # TODO: Subflow 6 from Tines:
        # TODO: Purge the deleted Zendesk users

        # Finally after all this we transition to the next stage

        raise NotImplementedError("phase-2 Request processing not implemented")

    def process_phase3(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        self._validate_request("phase-3", request)
        # TODO: Phase-3 from Tines
        # This is just checking if the customer still appears in the customer
        # API or not
        # TODO: What should we do if the previous erasure states all failed?

        # Finally transition to done
        raise NotImplementedError("phase-3 Request processing not implemented")

    def enrich_request(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
        """Enrich the erasure request if needed with details from other sources"""
        # Enrichment could include getting more details on the customer that
        # will be needed by downstream erasure processes

        if "email" not in request.erasure_details:
            raise MissingEmailAddress(request)

        # We will try and find details from all customer regions
        enrichment_data: list[CustomerDetails] = []
        enrichment_data.extend(
            self.customer_api.get_customer_details(
                email=request.erasure_details["email"]
            )
        )
        for site_id in ALL_SITE_IDS:
            enrichment_data.extend(
                self.customer_api.get_customer_details(
                    email=request.erasure_details["email"], site_id=site_id
                )
            )

        found_site_ids: set[int] = set()
        fields_added: list[str] = []
        for entry in enrichment_data:
            # Enrich with the first found entries
            # If the customer has used the email in multiple regions with
            # different details this might enrich with weird values.
            # Essentially this is a best effort enrichment as email should be
            # enough for most systems to erase by, the extra details are just
            # useful for debugging purposes.
            if entry.first_name and "first-name" not in request.erasure_details:
                request.erasure_details["first-name"] = entry.first_name
                fields_added.append("first-name")
            if entry.last_name and "last-name" not in request.erasure_details:
                request.erasure_details["last-name"] = entry.last_name
                fields_added.append("last-name")
            if entry.loyalty_number and "rfl" not in request.erasure_details:
                request.erasure_details["rfl"] = entry.loyalty_number
                fields_added.append("rfl")
            if entry.mobile_number and "phone-number" not in request.erasure_details:
                request.erasure_details["phone-number"] = entry.mobile_number
                fields_added.append("phone-number")
            if entry.site_id:
                found_site_ids.add(entry.site_id)

        audit_message: str | None = None
        if len(fields_added) > 0:
            fields_list = ",".join(fields_added)
            audit_message = (
                f"The following fields were added via enrichment: {fields_list}"
            )
        # Update site_ids
        current_site_ids = set(request.site_ids)
        if current_site_ids != found_site_ids:
            new_site_ids: list[int] = list(found_site_ids | current_site_ids)
            new_site_ids.sort()

file.content:
def test_archiver_exception_does_not_propagate(monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    archiver_mock = _make_archiver_mock()
    cast(MagicMock, archiver_mock.archive).side_effect = RuntimeError("S3 down")
    proc, repo = _build_processor(archiver=cast(AuditArchiver, archiver_mock))
    _seed_done_via_phase1(monkeypatch, proc)
    request = _make_request(state="phase-1")
    repo.create(request)

    proc.process()  # should not raise even though archiver fails


def test_process_request_without_archiver_does_not_raise(
    monkeypatch: MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    proc, repo = _build_processor(archiver=None)
    _seed_done_via_phase1(monkeypatch, proc)
    request = _make_request(state="phase-1")
    repo.create(request)

    proc.process()  # no archiver configured — must not raise

input.content:
# Plan — Address review findings M1, M2, M3, m8, m11, n5

## Context

The deep-code-reviewer flagged six issues on `DATA_4715_audit_log_to_s3` against
`master`. None are in the critical bucket, but two are deployment-time bugs
that would cause the new audit-log-to-S3 pipeline to silently misbehave in
production, and one is an API regression that can cause a successful create to
return 500. The other three are smaller correctness/visibility/hygiene
fix-ups.

This plan tightens those gaps before merge so the audit archival path is
trustworthy on the very first deploy that actually exercises it (whenever the
phase-3 handler lands), and so callers of `POST /erasure-request` cannot get a
spurious 5xx after the request has already been persisted.

## Scope and approach

### M1 — `_archive_if_done` has no live trigger; document and update TODO.md

`request_processor.py:195-212` already gates archival on `request.state ==
"done"`. No production handler currently transitions to `"done"` (`phase-2`
and `phase-3` raise `NotImplementedError` before any transition). The
plumbing is correct; the trigger is pending phase-3 implementation.

Changes:
- Expand the `_archive_if_done` docstring to spell out the precondition: it
  fires only when the just-completed `process_request` was the call that
  transitioned the request to `"done"` in this same iteration. Future
  maintainers who add a new terminal handler must call `_archive_if_done`
  themselves if the transition happens outside `process_request`.
- Update `TODO.md` `Add audit logging materialising to S3 after successful
  erasure` line to flag that the archival code is in place but is not yet
  triggered by any production handler — explicit reminder that phase-3 must
  end with a transition to `"done"`.

No regression test is added in this PR — there is no real "done" path to
exercise yet. A real end-to-end test will land alongside phase-3
implementation.

### M2 — Constrain `AuditLogS3Prefix` so the IAM ARN cannot break

`template.yaml:201,227` builds the resource ARN as
`arn:aws:s3:::${bucket}/${prefix}/*`. Empty prefix → `bucket//*` → no match
→ AccessDenied at runtime. The Python code (`audit_logging.py`) tolerates an
empty prefix, so the policy and the code disagree.

Changes (`template.yaml:30-32`):
- Add `MinLength: 1` and an `AllowedPattern` that forbids leading or trailing
  slashes to the `AuditLogS3Prefix` parameter. Tighten the `Description` to
  state the contract ("prefix within the bucket; must be non-empty; no
  leading or trailing slash").
- Optional: add `MinLength: 3`, `MaxLength: 63`, and the standard S3 bucket
  name `AllowedPattern` to `AuditLogS3Bucket` for symmetry.

This keeps the IAM ARNs at lines 201 and 227 unchanged — they are correct as
soon as the parameter constraint is enforced. We rely on parameter
validation rather than relaxing the ARN, so we do not over-grant S3
permissions on the bucket root.

### M3 — Make post-create audit logging non-fatal

`creator_lambda.py:165` calls `audit.log(...)` after the request has been
successfully persisted. If that call raises (transient DynamoDB hiccup), the
caller gets a 500 even though the request *was* created. The new 409
duplicate path makes this latent issue easier to hit: a client that retries
on 5xx will then see a 409 it cannot reconcile.

Changes (`creator_lambda.py:153-166`):
- Wrap only the `audit.log(...)` call in a `try/except Exception` and log the
  failure at WARNING via `logger.exception(...)`. The 200 response is still
  returned. The audit gap is acceptable and observable; a 500 on a
  successfully persisted request is not.
- Keep the existing `try` block that wraps `request_repo.create` exactly as
  it is — its `except Exception` returning 500 is correct: if the create
  itself fails, we have not persisted anything.

### m8 — Strengthen `test_archiver_exception_does_not_propagate`

`tests/test_request_processor.py:285-293` only asserts no exception escapes
from `proc.process()`. It would still pass if a refactor accidentally
skipped the archive call entirely.

Changes:
- Add `cast(MagicMock, archiver_mock.archive).assert_called_once_with("req-archive")`
  after the `proc.process()` call (mirrors line 251 in the success-path test
  in the same file).

### m11 — Emit a dry-run log in `_archive_if_done`

`request_processor.py:202-203` returns silently when `dry_run` is True.
Adjacent dry-run branches (`_transition_request`, `process_new`, etc.) emit
`DryRun: …` log lines. Match the convention.

Changes:
- Replace the bare `return` with `logger.info("DryRun: would archive audit
  log for %s", request.id); return` (only when `request.state == "done"`, so
  we don't log for every non-terminal request — i.e., move the dry-run check
  *after* the state check).

### n5 — Redact API Gateway ID in README example

`README.md:37` contains `omqlg03jma.execute-api.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com`,
which looks like a live API ID.

Changes:
- Replace the host with `<api-id>.execute-api.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com` in
  the curl example.

## Files to modify

- `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py` — docstring on
  `_archive_if_done` (M1), reorder dry-run + state checks and add dry-run
  log line (m11)
- `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py` — wrap post-create
  `audit.log` in try/except (M3)
- `template.yaml` — add `MinLength`/`AllowedPattern` on `AuditLogS3Prefix`
  parameter (M2)
- `tests/test_request_processor.py` — add `assert_called_once_with` in
  `test_archiver_exception_does_not_propagate` (m8)
- `TODO.md` — annotate that S3 archival has no live trigger yet (M1)
- `README.md` — redact API Gateway host (n5)

No new functions or files. No test-file or production-file restructuring.

## Existing utilities reused

- `logger.exception` / `logger.info` — already used throughout
  `request_processor.py` and `creator_lambda.py`; no new logging plumbing.
- `MagicMock.assert_called_once_with` — pattern already used in the same
  test file (`tests/test_request_processor.py:251`).
- SAM parameter `AllowedPattern` / `MinLength` — already used on other
  parameters across the team's templates.

## Verification

Run from the repo root:

1. `uv run basedpyright` — must report 0 errors / 0 warnings (changes are
   small and should not affect typing).
2. `uv run pytest` — all 173 existing tests plus the strengthened m8 test
   must pass. Specifically check:
   - `tests/test_request_processor.py::test_archiver_exception_does_not_propagate`
     — now asserts the archive call was attempted.
   - `tests/test_creator_lambda.py` — confirm no existing test expects a 500
     when `audit.log` raises (should be none; if one exists, update to
     expect 200 with the warn-log instead).
3. `sam validate --lint` — must pass; the new `AllowedPattern` /
   `MinLength` on `AuditLogS3Prefix` is standard SAM/CFN.
4. `sam build` — must complete successfully.
5. Manual sanity: read the diff and confirm no other call site of
   `audit.log` in the creator lambda's success path is left bare.

No deploy is part of this plan — `sam deploy` is human/CI-only per
`CLAUDE.md`.

## Out of scope (deferred)

- m1 (CloudWatch metric on archive failure) — useful but a larger change
  (new Powertools `Metrics` wiring); track separately.
- m9 (`AWS::IAM::Policy` vs `serverless-access-manager` reconciliation) —
  needs platform-team confirmation, not a code change.
- m2/m3/m4/m5/m6/m7/m10 and n1–n7 (other than n5) — minor/nit; defer.

input.plan:
# Plan — Address review findings M1, M2, M3, m8, m11, n5

## Context

The deep-code-reviewer flagged six issues on `DATA_4715_audit_log_to_s3` against
`master`. None are in the critical bucket, but two are deployment-time bugs
that would cause the new audit-log-to-S3 pipeline to silently misbehave in
production, and one is an API regression that can cause a successful create to
return 500. The other three are smaller correctness/visibility/hygiene
fix-ups.

This plan tightens those gaps before merge so the audit archival path is
trustworthy on the very first deploy that actually exercises it (whenever the
phase-3 handler lands), and so callers of `POST /erasure-request` cannot get a
spurious 5xx after the request has already been persisted.

## Scope and approach

### M1 — `_archive_if_done` has no live trigger; document and update TODO.md

`request_processor.py:195-212` already gates archival on `request.state ==
"done"`. No production handler currently transitions to `"done"` (`phase-2`
and `phase-3` raise `NotImplementedError` before any transition). The
plumbing is correct; the trigger is pending phase-3 implementation.

Changes:
- Expand the `_archive_if_done` docstring to spell out the precondition: it
  fires only when the just-completed `process_request` was the call that
  transitioned the request to `"done"` in this same iteration. Future
  maintainers who add a new terminal handler must call `_archive_if_done`
  themselves if the transition happens outside `process_request`.
- Update `TODO.md` `Add audit logging materialising to S3 after successful
  erasure` line to flag that the archival code is in place but is not yet
  triggered by any production handler — explicit reminder that phase-3 must
  end with a transition to `"done"`.

No regression test is added in this PR — there is no real "done" path to
exercise yet. A real end-to-end test will land alongside phase-3
implementation.

### M2 — Constrain `AuditLogS3Prefix` so the IAM ARN cannot break

`template.yaml:201,227` builds the resource ARN as
`arn:aws:s3:::${bucket}/${prefix}/*`. Empty prefix → `bucket//*` → no match
→ AccessDenied at runtime. The Python code (`audit_logging.py`) tolerates an
empty prefix, so the policy and the code disagree.

Changes (`template.yaml:30-32`):
- Add `MinLength: 1` and an `AllowedPattern` that forbids leading or trailing
  slashes to the `AuditLogS3Prefix` parameter. Tighten the `Description` to
  state the contract ("prefix within the bucket; must be non-empty; no
  leading or trailing slash").
- Optional: add `MinLength: 3`, `MaxLength: 63`, and the standard S3 bucket
  name `AllowedPattern` to `AuditLogS3Bucket` for symmetry.

This keeps the IAM ARNs at lines 201 and 227 unchanged — they are correct as
soon as the parameter constraint is enforced. We rely on parameter
validation rather than relaxing the ARN, so we do not over-grant S3
permissions on the bucket root.

### M3 — Make post-create audit logging non-fatal

`creator_lambda.py:165` calls `audit.log(...)` after the request has been
successfully persisted. If that call raises (transient DynamoDB hiccup), the
caller gets a 500 even though the request *was* created. The new 409
duplicate path makes this latent issue easier to hit: a client that retries
on 5xx will then see a 409 it cannot reconcile.

Changes (`creator_lambda.py:153-166`):
- Wrap only the `audit.log(...)` call in a `try/except Exception` and log the
  failure at WARNING via `logger.exception(...)`. The 200 response is still
  returned. The audit gap is acceptable and observable; a 500 on a
  successfully persisted request is not.
- Keep the existing `try` block that wraps `request_repo.create` exactly as
  it is — its `except Exception` returning 500 is correct: if the create
  itself fails, we have not persisted anything.

### m8 — Strengthen `test_archiver_exception_does_not_propagate`

`tests/test_request_processor.py:285-293` only asserts no exception escapes
from `proc.process()`. It would still pass if a refactor accidentally
skipped the archive call entirely.

Changes:
- Add `cast(MagicMock, archiver_mock.archive).assert_called_once_with("req-archive")`
  after the `proc.process()` call (mirrors line 251 in the success-path test
  in the same file).

### m11 — Emit a dry-run log in `_archive_if_done`

`request_processor.py:202-203` returns silently when `dry_run` is True.
Adjacent dry-run branches (`_transition_request`, `process_new`, etc.) emit
`DryRun: …` log lines. Match the convention.

Changes:
- Replace the bare `return` with `logger.info("DryRun: would archive audit
  log for %s", request.id); return` (only when `request.state == "done"`, so
  we don't log for every non-terminal request — i.e., move the dry-run check
  *after* the state check).

### n5 — Redact API Gateway ID in README example

`README.md:37` contains `omqlg03jma.execute-api.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com`,
which looks like a live API ID.

Changes:
- Replace the host with `<api-id>.execute-api.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com` in
  the curl example.

## Files to modify

- `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py` — docstring on
  `_archive_if_done` (M1), reorder dry-run + state checks and add dry-run
  log line (m11)
- `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py` — wrap post-create
  `audit.log` in try/except (M3)
- `template.yaml` — add `MinLength`/`AllowedPattern` on `AuditLogS3Prefix`
  parameter (M2)
- `tests/test_request_processor.py` — add `assert_called_once_with` in
  `test_archiver_exception_does_not_propagate` (m8)
- `TODO.md` — annotate that S3 archival has no live trigger yet (M1)
- `README.md` — redact API Gateway host (n5)

No new functions or files. No test-file or production-file restructuring.

## Existing utilities reused

- `logger.exception` / `logger.info` — already used throughout
  `request_processor.py` and `creator_lambda.py`; no new logging plumbing.
- `MagicMock.assert_called_once_with` — pattern already used in the same
  test file (`tests/test_request_processor.py:251`).
- SAM parameter `AllowedPattern` / `MinLength` — already used on other
  parameters across the team's templates.

## Verification

Run from the repo root:

1. `uv run basedpyright` — must report 0 errors / 0 warnings (changes are
   small and should not affect typing).
2. `uv run pytest` — all 173 existing tests plus the strengthened m8 test
   must pass. Specifically check:
   - `tests/test_request_processor.py::test_archiver_exception_does_not_propagate`
     — now asserts the archive call was attempted.
   - `tests/test_creator_lambda.py` — confirm no existing test expects a 500
     when `audit.log` raises (should be none; if one exists, update to
     expect 200 with the warn-log instead).
3. `sam validate --lint` — must pass; the new `AllowedPattern` /
   `MinLength` on `AuditLogS3Prefix` is standard SAM/CFN.
4. `sam build` — must complete successfully.
5. Manual sanity: read the diff and confirm no other call site of
   `audit.log` in the creator lambda's success path is left bare.

No deploy is part of this plan — `sam deploy` is human/CI-only per
`CLAUDE.md`.

## Out of scope (deferred)

- m1 (CloudWatch metric on archive failure) — useful but a larger change
  (new Powertools `Metrics` wiring); track separately.
- m9 (`AWS::IAM::Policy` vs `serverless-access-manager` reconciliation) —
  needs platform-team confirmation, not a code change.
- m2/m3/m4/m5/m6/m7/m10 and n1–n7 (other than n5) — minor/nit; defer.
